title: Wages by commodity - Canada.ca
content:
## Wages by agricultural commodity
- Fruits, vegetables (including canning/processing of these products if grown on the farm)
- Grains, oil seeds and seed corn
- Nursery-grown trees (including Christmas trees, greenhouses/nurseries)
- Pedigreed canola seed
Forthcoming minimum hourly wage rates for workers in Canada .
- Technician/worker = 1 or more years of apiary work experience
- Labourer = No apiary work experience, occupation

### Wages ($/hour) as of April 1, 2026
- Technician/worker: $18.34
- Technician/worker: $17.85
- Technician/worker: $16.12
- Technician/worker: $15.90

#### Newfoundland and Labrador
- Technician/worker: $16.35
- Technician/worker: $16.75
- Technician/worker: $17.60

#### Prince Edward Island
- Technician/worker: $17.00
- Technician/worker: $15.35
Quebec employers: Consult the website of the Ministère de  l'Immigration, de la Francisation et de l'Intégration (MIFI) (French only).

- Labourer (NOC 85101) = Job duties include picking, sorting, loading and cleaning mushrooms.
- Worker (NOC 85101 or 84120) = Job duties include picking, sorting, loading and cleaning mushrooms, as well as planting, examining mushrooms for quality and monitoring waterlines, airflow, humidity and temperature, and/or loading and unloading crops or containers of materials.
